name: Shortleaf Pine
summary: Near-extirpated PA native pine — listed by PA Natural Heritage Program as 'critically imperiled' in the state, with historical populations in south-central PA. A USFS Climate Change Tree Atlas projected winner. Restoring a missing structural element to the landscape.
notes: The northernmost extent of any major southern pine. Fire-adapted with the ability to sprout from the root collar after damage. Hardy Zone 6. Source seed from northernmost provenances (south-central PA, NJ Pine Barrens edge) for cold-hardiness. Susceptible to littleleaf disease on poorly-drained sites — site selection matters. Pair with pitch pine on dry ridges as a structural complement.

refs.label: PA Natural Heritage — Shortleaf Pine factsheet

planting.aspect: Well-drained dry slopes; rocky uplands; ridge tops
planting.soil: Acidic; sandy or rocky; well-drained sandstone-derived
planting.site_notes: Climate-trial planting — plant 50–100 trees in a single well-drained ridge or slope location to test site suitability. Avoid wet flats (littleleaf disease risk). Tube protection. Source from PA Department of Forestry or northern-edge provenance nurseries.
